details: :edit {file} opens a file for editing
Write first or use :edit! if you want to discard current changes
:find searches Vim's 'path' and edits the match
Useful when the file is not in the current directory
:browse opens the file-open dialog
Use it when you want to pick a file instead of typing its name
:buffers, :files, and :ls list known files/buffers
Use the list before switching with :buffer, :bn, :bp, or Ctrl-^

tips.summary: Jump to a listed buffer with :buffer {N}
details: :buffer {N} jumps to the numbered buffer from the buffer list
Use it after :ls when you know exactly which file you want

tips.summary: Step through the argument list with :next / :previous
details: :next moves to the next file in the argument list
:previous moves back to the earlier one

tips.summary: Jump to a specific argument with :argument {N}
details: :argument {N} edits the Nth file in the argument list
Use it when you know exactly which argument-list file you want

tips.summary: Jump to the ends of the argument list with :first / :last
details: :first edits the first file in the argument list
:last edits the last file in the argument list
Jumps to the alternate file
Great for back-and-forth edits
Ctrl-^ is Ctrl-6 on many keyboards
:bn goes to the next buffer
:bp goes to the previous buffer
:bd closes the current buffer/editor tab
Use it when you are done with the file
:e reloads if no changes
:e! discards changes and reloads
:w writes the file
In IdeaVim, this maps to IDE save

tips.summary: Write a copy to another file :w {file}
details: :w {file} writes the current buffer to a different file
Useful when you want a copy without changing the current buffer name
:wa writes all modified buffers
In IDE, typically saves all open editors
:wnext writes the current file and moves to the next one
:wprevious writes and moves to the previous file

tips.summary: Read a file into the current buffer with :read {file}
details: :read {file} inserts another file below the current line
Useful when you want to pull file contents into the current buffer
:q closes if no unsaved changes
:q! forces close/discard (if allowed)
:wq writes and quits
:x writes only if changed
ZZ also writes and quits the current file
:qa exits unless there are changed buffers
:qall closes all editors
Use the force form only if you really want to discard pending changes
:wqa writes changed buffers and exits
:xa is the equivalent all-files write-and-exit command
